signals.title: NZD/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + NFP Shock
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5784 — USD outperformance (avg +0.813%) vs NZD underperformance (avg -1.019%) creates a 1.833% G8 spread, the widest in the complex. The move is driven by the +150bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25%) and a strong US NFP report that has repriced Fed hike expectations. NZD/USD retail sentiment is 89% long, diverging from the -27,797 COT LF net short, signaling extreme retail capitulation risk if the 0.5780 support fails.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Intervention Risk + Vol
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.21 — the pair holds above the critical 160.00 level following a bear steepener shift in the US yield curve (2s10s +55.6bp). While the +289bp OIS carry (SOFR 3.62% vs TONA 0.73%) sustains the bid, VIX at 21.5 and a -1.10 25d RR put skew signal institutional hedging against BoJ intervention. JPY COT LF net short remains extreme at -105,136, leaving the pair vulnerable to a rapid unwind if the BoJ signals a hike on June 16.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + CESI
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7027 — IV at 22.2% vs HV30 at 9.1% creates a massive +13.1% Vol Risk Premium, indicating the options market is pricing in significant downside protection. This aligns with a -0.80 25d RR put skew and a negative AUD CESI (-8 misses), which contradicts the crowded COT LF net long of +56,800 (76.5% long). This positioning-data divergence suggests a high risk of a long-unwind toward 0.6950.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Vol Premium + Real Rate Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1517 — IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.4% (+2.7% VRP) reflects rising hedging demand ahead of the June 11 ECB meeting. Despite a +175bp nominal carry advantage for USD (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%), the real rate differential is narrower at 1.49% (USD +1.23% vs EUR -0.26%). DXY vs SPX correlation at -0.496 (z=+1.69σ) confirms USD is currently trading as a pure risk-off beneficiary amid the 2.64% S&P 500 drawdown.
